quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: In Hungary, Independence Day is celebrated on March 15th, commemorating the 1848 Revolution. Hungarians traditionally wear rosettes with the national colors of red, white, and green, and recite poetry by Sándor Petőfi, the revolutionary poet who inspired the uprising.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Olympic Peninsula's Banana Slugs
short_read.teaser: These bright yellow mollusks are nature's recyclers, helping forests thrive. Learn why they're so important to the Pacific Northwest ecosystem you see every day.

medium_read.title: Gray Whales Return to the Strait of Juan de Fuca
medium_read.teaser: Every summer, gray whales make the journey along the Olympic Peninsula coastline to feed in these nutrient-rich waters. Discover what brings them here and where you might spot them from shore.

long_read.title: The Remarkable History of Olympic National Park
long_read.teaser: From ancient rainforests to presidential proclamations, the story of how Olympic National Park became protected is filled with passionate advocates and close calls. This is the fascinating tale of preserving one of America's most diverse landscapes.

port_angeles.title: Black-Tailed Deer in Hurricane Ridge
port_angeles.description: Hurricane Ridge is home to the Olympic black-tailed deer, a subspecies unique to the Pacific Northwest. These gentle creatures are often spotted grazing in the meadows, especially in early morning and evening. They're smaller than other deer species and have adapted beautifully to mountain life.

puzzle_1.question: The Strait of Juan de Fuca is approximately ___ miles wide at its widest point.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Perfect! You know your local waters well, Barbara!
puzzle_1.close_response: Very close! The Strait is about 15 miles wide at its widest point.
puzzle_1.wrong_response: The Strait of Juan de Fuca is about 15 miles wide at its widest point — quite a span of beautiful water!
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: The Strait of Juan de Fuca connects the Pacific Ocean to Puget Sound and forms part of the border between Washington State and British Columbia. It was named after a Greek navigator who sailed for Spain in 1592.
puzzle_1.learn_more_label: More about the Strait — National Park Service

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'beautiful'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Szép! You remembered your Hungarian beautifully, Ildiko!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The word 'szép' means beautiful in Hungarian — such a lovely-sounding word!
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is unique among European languages as part of the Finno-Ugric language family, related to Finnish and Estonian rather than neighboring Slavic or Germanic languages. It has 14 vowels and uses extensive suffixes to modify word meanings.

puzzle_3.question: Which animal is known to use tools to open shellfish?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Wonderful! Sea otters are brilliant little creatures who use rocks to crack open their meals — you might see them floating in the kelp beds off the coast!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's the sea otter! These clever animals float on their backs and use rocks as hammers to crack open clams and mussels — one of the few marine mammals to use tools.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Sea otters have the densest fur of any mammal, with up to one million hairs per square inch to keep them warm in cold Pacific waters. They eat about 25% of their body weight each day and play a crucial role in maintaining healthy kelp forests.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
